title: What is a data structure?
body: A *data structure* is a deliberate way of organizing data so certain operations are fast. You already know two: lists and dicts. From here you'll build your own as *classes* — a class is a blueprint (`class Stack:`) and each `Stack()` you create from it is an object carrying its own data. Inside a class, functions are called *methods*, and their first parameter `self` means "this particular object". The next challenges have you build the classics — stack, queue, linked list — the exact structures CS courses and interviews assume you've touched.
